posts.author: Siddharth_Raghuraman
posts.text: Irritating upper circuit and price protection range — On kite level LPP - Please don’t do this. LPPs are a ridiculous concept as it is. More often than not LPP is preventing genuine market behavior than helping anything. Never seen a single person saying LPP helped them but you will see hundreds of posts about how LPP is setting incorrect limits

posts.author: Adarsh_Patil
posts.text: Irritating upper circuit and price protection range — Yeah, on it. Feeds on Kite may take a little more time. Also, we are working on Kite-level LPP and theoretical price range level restrictions to avoid any irregular executions.

posts.author: Siddharth_Raghuraman
posts.text: Irritating upper circuit and price protection range — Can this LPP value be sent in the quotes/websocket itself? then traders can avoid placing orders for these in the first place

posts.author: Adarsh_Patil
posts.text: Irritating upper circuit and price protection range — For now, you will need to parse the error message. Catch the rejection string, extract the limit value using regex, and then retry your order. Also, noted your feedback

posts.author: bkt
posts.text: Irritating upper circuit and price protection range — @Adarsh_Patil , please suggest how to automate this reliably if we want to retry. Should we parse this message, extract the LPP limit and then retry? I hope you would agree that relying on error message is fragile when real money is involved. Can you give us exact error code for this scenario and give the LPP limit as a proper field in JSON response?

posts.author: Adarsh_Patil
posts.text: Irritating upper circuit and price protection range — {D8C2B453-FAA4-442F-A6AE-9C3C766BFB82} 1654×512 66 KB Update: LLP range rejection is now live. If an order is rejected due to the LLP range, you will see the exact LLP in the rejection message itself.
